prompt_en: A match, when quickly struck against the phosphorus-coated paper, first produces a friction sound, then suddenly ignites with a "puff" noise. This occurs because the heat generated by friction ignites the chemical substances in the match head, triggering a rapid oxidation reaction and releasing gas.
prompt_vision: A match being struck against phosphorus-coated paper, then igniting with a small flame.
prompt_audio: Friction sound followed by a sudden 'puff' noise.

This occurs because the internal stress within the snowpack exceeds its strength limit, causing brittle fracture and releasing energy that generates sound waves and motion.
